top_signals.title: Bond vigilantes likely to force Warsh into hawkish pivot, strategists say
top_signals.summary: Bond yields surged on Kevin Warsh’s first day as Federal Reserve chair, with strategists at Yardeni Research and Bank of America warning that markets are losing patience with the central bank’s easing bias and that a hawkish pivot is increasingly inevitable. Yardeni Research said the bond market selloff reflects fears that Warsh will tolerate inflation ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: India, EU eye collaboration to build energy supply chains, port infrastructure: Modi
top_signals.summary: India is accelerating efforts to build resilient energy supply chains by collaborating with global partners to soften the blow from geopolitical conflicts and the energy crisis that are threatening economies across the globe, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi said May 16. “First came COVID-19, and then came wars, and now an energy crisis. top_signals.title: ZESTAs Launches Global Liquid Hydrogen Alliance to Accelerate Zero-Emission Shipping
top_signals.summary: The Zero Emissions Ship Technology Association (ZESTAs) today launched the Global Liquid Hydrogen Alliance (Alliance), a new international platform dedicated to advancing pure green hydrogen and liquid hydrogen (LH2) as a deployable, scalable, and commercially viable zero-emission fuel for international maritime shipping. The Alliance launches as the transition moves from aspiration to deployment. At MEPC ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Political and Economic Shifts Cause Massive 42% Clean Energy Slump
top_signals.summary: The world’s largest two economies, the United States and China, are both majorly cooling off on investments in clean energy manufacturing, but for very different reasons. In China, the decline in investing from peak levels in 2023 reflects a market correction after years of oversupply and a slowdown in economic growth. In the United States, the trend comes as a reflection of shifting policy priorities and the private sector’s reaction to political uncertainty in the Trump era. The result is a slowdown in clean energy manufacturing investing…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Ship managers face rising litigation exposure as US$2.5 million claim highlights risk of inherited ship conditions
top_signals.summary: Ship managers are facing increasing litigation risks that are linked to inherited ship conditions, highlighted after a recent incident in which claims exceeding US$2.5 million were brought against a ship manager after taking over a defective ship. The claim, which was ultimately reduced to just US$250,000 after it was successfully supported by International Transport Intermediaries ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Shipbuilding surge lifts HJ Shipbuilding & Construction profit in Q1
top_signals.summary: HJ Shipbuilding & Construction said on the 18th that it posted first-quarter consolidation sales of 541.4 billion won and operating profit of 24.6 billion won. Compared with a year earlier, sales rose 32% and operating profit jumped 350.7%. Net profit was 25.5 billion won, up 359.2% from a year earlier. The operating margin increased to ...

top_signals.title: Hydroniq Coolers to equip newbuild wellboat
top_signals.summary: Norway-based Hydroniq Coolers has been contracted by Norwegian shipbuilder Fitjar Mekaniske Verksted to deliver its hull-integrated marine cooling system to a live fish carrier that is being built for North Salmon Service. Hydroniq Coolers’ scope of supply includes a complete “Rack” seawater cooling system, which is integrated in the hull below the main engine room ...

top_signals.title: Singapore conventional bunker sales declined around 8% in April
top_signals.summary: Lower sales of VLSFO, HSFO and MGO drove an 8% month-on-month decline in the port’s total bunker sales in April. Changes in bunker sales from March to April: Total sales down 390,000 mt to 4.23 million mt VLSFO sales down 147,000 mt to 2.19 million mt HSFO sales down 147,000 mt to 1.79 million mt ...
